110380.explanation: Resident participation is important, and the method of participation must be evaluated per situation. The elected municipal council remains ultimately responsible.

110383.explanation: We support more space for other business activities around farms, but only if it fits the environment. So it will not be allowed without consideration.

110384.explanation: Local initiatives contribute to sustainability and community spirit.

110385.explanation: Berkelland takes responsibility for refugees in need of help, with attention to support and the environment. This should be evaluated with the community at the end of the period before deciding to continue or close.

110386.explanation: Employers must take responsibility for housing foreign workers, but this does not always have to be on their own property.

110390.explanation: Not just one fireworks show due to the spread of the various villages in the municipality. Leave the organization to initiatives from the community.

110391.explanation: Costs for waste separation must remain manageable for residents. Free collection of usable bulky waste by thrift stores works well and should be maintained.

110393.explanation: Accessibility is important and it is good that the municipality encourages entrepreneurs.

110394.explanation: Do not allow homes on agricultural land without careful consideration. Build as much as possible in and around the villages. The countryside is for our agricultural entrepreneurs.

110398.explanation: Culture and music education contribute to broad development.

110399.explanation: Associations are the heart of the community.

110402.explanation: More municipal enforcement officers does not lead to more safety.

110403.explanation: More camera surveillance helps in tracking perpetrators after incidents.

110405.explanation: Residents should be able to continue living in their own municipality.

110407.explanation: This undermines the touristic character and attracts people from outside the municipality.

110408.explanation: Expansion may be necessary, preserving the landscape, in accordance with the zoning plan and environmental vision. For example, through 'Red for Red' (Rood voor Rood).

110410.explanation: The municipality must protect vulnerable residents.

110411.explanation: Raising the income threshold to receive help from the municipality is good to assist more vulnerable people. An important group can currently just miss out on getting help.

110413.explanation: Preparing for the future is necessary.

110414.explanation: Set the maximum speed limit within built-up areas based on the local situation. Not automatically reduce to 30 km/h.

110415.explanation: Choice in care is a good goal because care should suit the individual. However, the practical and financial implementation is not always feasible if there must always be a choice for a care provider that fits faith, culture, or beliefs.

110416.explanation: Quality is paramount in youth care, while also considering efficiency and cost.

110417.explanation: Burden on residents and entrepreneurs must remain limited if financially possible.

110426.explanation: Regional language is part of the identity, but the municipality should not obligate schools.

110427.explanation: The CDA supports a cautious drug policy.

110429.explanation: In large villages where appropriate, high-rise buildings may be allowed to solve housing shortages. No tall skyscrapers; a maximum of 5 floors.

110431.explanation: It is important that homes and shops remain accessible.

110434.explanation: With attention to the landscape and residents’ health, the municipality can cooperate with wind turbine initiatives.

110380.explanation: As residents, we govern our own municipality. For major decisions and/or topics that play a big role, a referendum can be a nice and fair way to make a decision, provided people are well informed in advance. We are also open to other forms of co-decision making.

110383.explanation: We support sustainability in agriculture and the protection of landscape and nature. At the same time, farmers must be able to earn money with their business. A shop or campsite can help, provided there is attention to the environment and nature. Therefore, the municipality never automatically grants permission.

110384.explanation: This is the ideal picture: residents jointly generate their own green and affordable electricity with a wind turbine or solar panels. They use the electricity in the same location, so the grid is not burdened. Therefore, we want to stimulate energy cooperatives-not only financially but also with knowledge and advice.

110385.explanation: Good reception and integration of newcomers is our responsibility. GroenLinks-PvdA supports an inclusive municipality where everyone can participate and feel at home.

110386.explanation: Those who bring workers to our municipality must ensure that these workers have decent housing, without causing inconvenience to our residents. There is no place in our municipality for exploitation and nuisance.

110390.explanation: The best New Year's celebration is celebrated together. GroenLinks-PvdA welcomes the fireworks ban starting in 2026. If the municipality organizes one big show, we can keep New Year's Night safe for everyone. For the environment, climate, and health, it should be a show with electronic fireworks as far as we are concerned.

110393.explanation: Accessibility is vital for people with disabilities. Many important places are not yet well accessible, for example, for someone in a wheelchair. Entrepreneurs often cannot afford such adjustments on their own. Therefore, we believe the municipality should step in.

110394.explanation: In Berkelland, we want building primarily in the villages, close to facilities. In our beautiful countryside, reusing existing buildings always takes priority over new construction.

110398.explanation: Music lessons must be accessible to every child. Music provides calm, stimulates creativity, and is good for mental health. Therefore, we support subsidies for music education for primary school children. This way, we offer every child equal opportunities to engage with culture and art.

110399.explanation: Sports give a lot of energy and satisfaction. It keeps you healthy and the sports club is a meeting place. If a sports club fails, an entire neighborhood suffers. Therefore, we believe the municipality should step in if necessary.

110400.explanation: Our monuments make our municipality beautiful and unique. We believe they need proper care. It would be a great shame if unique places are lost forever because we choose to cut budgets now.

110402.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA chooses a society where everyone feels safe. Safety is more than enforcement: it is also prevention, protection, and connection. That is exactly what community enforcement officers (BOAs) do, and we want more of such people in society.

110403.explanation: People on the street can supervise much better than cameras. Safety does not come from technology and remote surveillance but from a neighborhood police officer or community enforcement officer who addresses people and fosters a sense of protection and connection.

110405.explanation: Giving priority to a certain group based on origin is discrimination. Article 1 of the Constitution prohibits this.

110407.explanation: Holiday homes do not solve the housing shortage. They are located in places unsuitable for permanent residence. They are often poorly accessible and far from shops and other facilities. We must mainly ensure quick housing solutions within villages or possibly at their edges.

110408.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to build first within the built-up area and only expand outside if there is no other option. We want to keep the villages sufficiently livable and not fill the countryside with buildings.

110410.explanation: If debt forgiveness of municipal taxes stops, people with the lowest incomes in our municipality will get into big trouble. They simply cannot miss that money. We believe everyone should be able to keep their head above water, which is why we support debt forgiveness.

110411.explanation: Assistance should also be available just above the minimum, as more and more people are facing serious financial problems, including those with an income slightly above the legal social minimum.

110413.explanation: Electric driving is the future. We will provide more charging points in public spaces at parking lots.

110414.explanation: Although slower traffic is usually safer, the optimal speed limit depends on the traffic situation. We support neighborhood initiatives for traffic safety.

110415.explanation: Good care is paramount. We stand for an inclusive municipality where everyone feels at home, regardless of religion or background. It is important that everyone can receive care from a healthcare provider. Care personnel are scarce and demand for care is increasing. Therefore, collaboration is essential.

110416.explanation: The waiting lists and problems in youth assistance and youth care are enormous. You don't solve this by further budget cuts and always choosing the cheapest option. We must mainly ensure certainty and provide help that matches the concerns of our youth.

110417.explanation: No party supports raising taxes. It's about what the municipality does with tax money for society. Increasing property tax can keep important facilities available to everyone in the municipality. We believe in principle: the strongest shoulders carry the heaviest burdens.

110426.explanation: Regional language is part of our culture. We want to keep the regional language alive but emphasize that schools determine their own curricula. Local politics has little influence on this.

110427.explanation: At the moment, there is no coffeeshop in Berkelland. GroenLinks-PvdA considers the establishment of a coffeeshop in the municipality undesirable. We are committed to a clean generation.

110429.explanation: We need to look at possibilities within different centers, because we do not want to overbuild the rural area while keeping the centers attractive for the future. The construction of affordable apartment buildings fits this approach.

110431.explanation: In designing plans, the party applies the STOMP principle, where walking, cycling, and public transport are prioritized over the private car. Shopping areas are primarily for pedestrians and cyclists.

110434.explanation: We aim for a fossil-free municipality by 2040 at the latest, with large-scale generation from wind and solar as central elements. The municipality must accelerate the Regional Energy Strategy. We support local control in sustainable energy.

110380.explanation: Our program chooses strong representative democracy and participation through council and involvement, not binding referenda as a decision-making tool.

110383.explanation: Farmers must have space for diversification such as shops or campsites, provided it is appropriate and with limited regulations. The principle is: enable, not block. This keeps the countryside livable and economically strong.

110384.explanation: The municipality stimulates energy cooperatives and innovation but chooses affordable, practical solutions without extra subsidies or high burdens for residents.

110385.explanation: If intake decreases and procedures are faster, shelters no longer need to remain open unnecessarily. We close them as soon as possible, without leaving people out in the cold.

110386.explanation: Employers take responsibility. Housing on the farmyard prevents pressure on villages and keeps homes available for starters and families. This fits with space for entrepreneurship.

110390.explanation: The program advocates fewer rules, support for local events, and no unnecessary municipal expenses. A mandatory municipal fireworks display does not fit with this.

110391.explanation: The program opts for affordable waste collection fees and more efficient collection, not for free extra services such as curbside bulky waste pickup.

110393.explanation: VVD Berkelland chooses fewer rules, low burdens, and personal responsibility. Direct municipal financial support to entrepreneurs does not fit with that.

110394.explanation: VVD supports repurposing farmyards but no unrestricted new construction: housing must fit the landscape and village character. Only within existing farms, not just on agricultural land.

110398.explanation: The program favors targeted support via a sports and culture pass, not broad music lesson subsidies. Talent development also lies with education; freedom of choice and personal responsibility are paramount and align with low costs.

110399.explanation: Sports clubs belong to and are for residents. The municipality facilitates and invests in facilities, but structurally providing financial support is inconsistent with personal responsibility.

110400.explanation: Our program emphasizes the importance of culture and monuments for identity, wellbeing, and economy. Budget cuts on monument care do not fit with this.

110402.explanation: The program focuses on visible and nearby enforcement. To be genuinely present in all centers and effectively act against nuisance and crime, more enforcement officers (boa's) are needed.

110403.explanation: We are not in favor of more camera surveillance in general, but only for targeted and temporary monitoring of risk locations. Safety requires tailor-made approaches, not standard expansion.

110405.explanation: Our housing construction is primarily intended for residents of Berkelland. We strengthen ties with the municipality and prevent scarce social rental housing from being at the expense of local home seekers.

110407.explanation: Holiday homes are for recreation. Permanent residence undermines tourism, but in an acute housing crisis, a temporary, strict tolerance construction can be justifiable.

110408.explanation: We want to build at the edges of centers and limitedly in the countryside to solve the housing shortage and keep villages vital.

110410.explanation: Our program focuses on poverty reduction and accessibility. Waivers prevent people with low income from getting into deeper trouble.

110411.explanation: The subsistence minimum is already set at 130%. Raising the income limit above that makes poverty policy too broad and less focused on those who truly need help.

110413.explanation: We anticipate zero-emission mobility and want to ensure sufficient charging stations. This also requires more parking spaces suitable for charging.

110414.explanation: We are for more traffic safety but not for a generic 30 km/h limit. Speed should be determined per road based on safety and function.

110415.explanation: The program opts for affordable, accessible, and efficient care. Unlimited freedom of choice based on religion or conviction is not mentioned and does not fit cost control.

110416.explanation: Through efficient processes and smarter purchasing, the municipality can achieve a competitive price while maintaining quality, so youth care remains affordable and available. The focus is explicitly on the best price-quality ratio. Quality is guaranteed through contract agreements and supervision, but cost control is leading.

110417.explanation: VVD wants to keep burdens for residents as low as possible. If finances allow and waste is prevented, lowering property tax is appropriate.

110426.explanation: Our program values the Achterhoek identity but does not prescribe municipal obligations for language education. Schools decide this themselves.

110427.explanation: By allowing a coffeeshop under regulation, the municipality maintains control over soft drugs, limits illegality, and increases safety and control.

110429.explanation: Our program chooses housing construction that fits the village character. We focus on dispersion, small scale, and appropriate heights, not tall apartment buildings.

110431.explanation: Our program does not call for more parking spaces everywhere but for sufficient and appropriate parking per center, neighborhood, and downtown, tailored to local needs.

110434.explanation: We are against windmills in Berkelland. Only with demonstrable support do we cooperate. By participating in policy-making, we maintain control, set limits, and protect the landscape; if we don't, the province takes over control.

110383.explanation: If this is the farmer's wish, then it should be possible. Moreover, it contributes to our wonderful sense of community.

110384.explanation: We are neutral on this. If residents want to do this, a small budget is appropriate. But then money must also be allocated for wood stoves! People should have the choice of how to heat their homes; both options should be supported.

110385.explanation: Agreed. And no expansion or construction of new asylum centers/refugee shelters. Refugees should be accommodated in their own region neighboring the area they fled from. If we cannot prevent this, then we prefer only women, children, and families.

110386.explanation: It may be offered, but it is up to the seasonal worker themselves.

110390.explanation: It is a tradition for residents to set off fireworks themselves; we grant them this freedom. However, it must be done safely.

110391.explanation: This should be facilitated twice a year.

110393.explanation: They do help up to the entrance, so the sidewalk and access. The rest, inside, is up to the entrepreneur.

110394.explanation: This must be 100% possible. Think of inheriting properties and homes for family.

110398.explanation: Children should be given the opportunity to take music lessons, even if this is not feasible from home.

110399.explanation: Associations are the foundation of our community. But only if the bookkeeping is in order!

110400.explanation: It must be kept in good condition; a piece of culture and history must be preserved. But no unnecessary money should be spent here.

110402.explanation: We are in favor of more neighborhood police officers.

110403.explanation: No, we do not want a surveillance state; too much camera monitoring causes this. We are concerned about privacy. Shops have a free choice in this, but the streets should not be filled with cameras. We do not want mass surveillance through excessive camera monitoring.

110407.explanation: If there is a housing shortage, this must be allowed and should not be fined.

110408.explanation: Village boundaries do not have to be limits. The municipality should cooperate with good initiatives.

110410.explanation: Disagree, we think this is a good arrangement.

110411.explanation: If this is necessary to provide breathing space for families who just fall outside this boundary, then certainly.

110413.explanation: The amounts allocated for this are ridiculously high. If a company wants to install a pole, fine. But not the municipality.

110414.explanation: We do think that more neighborhoods and school zones should be designated as residential areas to lower speeds.

110415.explanation: Agreed, provided the costs are not more than 25% of normal care costs.

110426.explanation: The charm of the Achterhoek region must be preserved; this is good for cultural development, for example, a weekly half-hour in the Achterhoek dialect.

110427.explanation: Agreed. We believe this reduces crime.

110429.explanation: Yes, but a maximum of four stories. Our Berkelland landscape is recognizable for its low-rise buildings and authentic details. Too high apartment blocks do not do our landscape any favors. Throughout Berkelland, there are several apartment buildings, almost all four stories high. We believe more such buildings can be constructed.

110431.explanation: Existing parking spaces should be improved for more room. And mother-child parking spots should be created.

110434.explanation: Definitely disagree. These are major polluters.

110380.explanation: BBB supports citizen participation and consultation instruments at the local level so that the residents of Berkelland can make their voices heard on important themes.

110383.explanation: Every form of entrepreneurship in rural areas should be made easier. Of course, without adverse impact on farmers, other entrepreneurs, and local residents.

110384.explanation: BBB supports sustainability but opposes plans that are too expensive or have little effect. Those in debt cannot afford to be green. We support citizen initiatives in this area, but not financially.

110385.explanation: BBB wants to significantly reduce the asylum influx, but Berkelland itself has limited influence on this. We accommodate genuine refugees because it is legally required.

110386.explanation: Employers with seasonal workers have a moral responsibility to provide housing and leisure activities for these employees. This does not necessarily have to be on their own company premises, but should be nearby.

110390.explanation: Fireworks should be legal for private individuals. We leave the organization of fireworks shows to associations that can easily obtain a permit for this. The municipality should not spend money on fireworks.

110391.explanation: All initiatives to collect bulky waste free of charge from residents, such as Bohero, should be continuously supported by the municipality of Berkelland. This is currently well arranged.

110393.explanation: Accessibility for people with disabilities should be a matter of course. However, entrepreneurs themselves are primarily responsible for this.

110394.explanation: BBB wants to significantly simplify housing construction regulations. Building must happen. BBB focuses on 'Red for Red', creating residential yards, splitting homes, adding stories, and in-fill locations. Anything must be possible as long as it doesn't get out of hand.

110398.explanation: Music is relevant, but money can only be spent once. BBB prefers to invest in swimming lessons or improving educational quality.

110399.explanation: Associations can organize all kinds of activities to survive financially. Permits and rules should not be hindrances in this. The municipality should facilitate, not finance. Current subsidy schemes for, among others, youth are fine and should be maintained.

110400.explanation: Monuments are valuable, but money can only be spent once.

110402.explanation: Safety is an important asset. BOAs (special enforcement officers) must be visible and approachable. However, the number of civil servants should not keep increasing endlessly.

110403.explanation: BBB is against camera surveillance. We choose to trust rather than mistrust.

110405.explanation: Housing is first allocated to people with a strong local connection. BBB advocates for a suitable policy in allocating social housing and selling new construction projects accordingly.

110407.explanation: Everything that can currently counteract the tightness in the housing market must be seized.

110408.explanation: BBB primarily advocates for infill locations, rooftop additions, and splitting homes within the built-up area. In the rural area, we focus on 'Red for Red' (building permits in exchange for demolition) and creating residential yards. Afterwards, we will look at expanding villages beyond the current borders.

110410.explanation: Every resident must contribute by paying taxes. If there is no financial room, allowances are available.

110411.explanation: This threshold was recently raised. Further increase is not necessary.

110413.explanation: Charging points for electric cars take up parking spaces for other cars. Private individuals can provide more charging points if needed. The current number of spots is sufficient.

110414.explanation: The current speeds are fine. Desired changes should be reviewed per situation.

110415.explanation: Care providers must always provide care regardless of religion, culture, or beliefs.

110416.explanation: The right price-quality ratio must be considered. Contracts with external parties such as Fijnder, the regional work-learning company, should be continuously evaluated, especially financially.

110417.explanation: Taxes in Berkelland keep rising. BBB wants to reverse this. Cost savings by focusing on core tasks and more efficient operations are urgently needed.

110426.explanation: BBB is proud of our Achterhoek dialect. It connects our residents and is a piece of cultural heritage. The municipality should promote attention to this language.

110427.explanation: If we can avoid this, we would prefer that. The chance of nuisance from the presence of a coffee shop is high.

110429.explanation: BBB supports rooftop additions. Various apartment complexes may well be made higher.

110431.explanation: The current number is good. No more, no less.

110434.explanation: BBB is against wind turbines and wind turbines along the German border. Our sustainable agricultural land is far too vulnerable for this, and meadow birds will abandon Berkelland en masse.

110380.explanation: Only for very weighty decisions such as a municipal reorganization.

110383.explanation: It depends on the type of store or campsite and the location. Much is already possible and present.

110384.explanation: There are already possibilities for this.

110385.explanation: For shelter locations, agreements have been made with neighbors regarding maximum number of years and residents. If there is reason, closure may be considered earlier.

110386.explanation: On private property this is not always possible, but residents are responsible themselves.

110390.explanation: It can be an option; sometimes you have to create new traditions. This depends on costs and interest from residents.

110391.explanation: This is currently arranged; we have not received signals that this should change.

110393.explanation: Accessibility for everyone is important, but entrepreneurs must arrange this for their own businesses.

110394.explanation: There are certainly possibilities for living on a farmyard, but there are rules and limits attached.

110398.explanation: The municipality ensures that children can get acquainted with music and other cultural forms. Continuing education usually follows through music associations or privately. Various subsidies are already available, especially for parents who cannot afford it.

110399.explanation: There are already possibilities for this, but it is not unlimited.

110400.explanation: Hardly any money goes to this now; it should actually be more. We want to preserve the few monuments we still have for generations to come.

110402.explanation: BOAs are regularly visible in public spaces and contribute to safety. Additional police are currently a more important and broad desire in the region.

110403.explanation: In places where it is necessary and where problems frequently occur, such as at a fair.

110405.explanation: Give as much priority as possible to local residents in the housing market. We have already submitted and had accepted a motion on this regarding home buying.

110407.explanation: This must be regulated nationally; we conform to national legislation. However, in anticipation of national legislation, temporarily more can be allowed given the emergency in the housing market.

110408.explanation: The towns will have to be expanded to solve the housing shortage. Infill cannot be unlimited and is not always desirable. You also need to preserve some green space in the towns.

110410.explanation: Always keep looking at whether it can be reduced.

110411.explanation: However, it has recently already been adjusted!

110413.explanation: Electric driving will eventually increase more and more.

110414.explanation: In residential and central areas, this is the desirable standard. For some roads, a higher speed is desirable.

110415.explanation: Government care must be independent of religion, culture, or beliefs.

110416.explanation: Price is very important, but quality is too. Always ensure that cheap does not become expensive in the end.

110417.explanation: Current levels should possibly only be increased by inflation at most.

110426.explanation: We consider regional dialect important. Other municipalities in the Achterhoek pay much more attention to this. Regional dialect deserves more attention in Berkelland as well.

110427.explanation: Doetinchem and Enschede have a regional function in this regard and are also equipped to handle all related matters.

110429.explanation: Berkelland must retain its rural character; high residential towers do not fit here. There are sufficient other housing development options.

110431.explanation: Sometimes necessary, but generally cannot be answered with a yes or no.

110434.explanation: Berkelland also has a task to generate sustainable energy. Cooperation on wind turbines could be the case if the new national standards and local policies are met.

statements.title: The municipality of Berkelland should cooperate in the construction of wind turbines.

titleParts.text: De gemeente Berkelland moet meewerken aan de bouw van

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.

statements.title: Residents of Berkelland should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Berkelland moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The municipality should cut spending on heritage conservation.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
The care of historic or special old buildings.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
Berkelland should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
There should be more camera surveillance in Berkelland.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: More high-rise residential buildings should be allowed in Berkelland.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
There should be more parking spaces in the town centers.

titleParts.text: Er moeten meer parkeerplaatsen komen in de

titleParts.glossary: A village or small town that is part of the municipality.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to include the Achterhoeks dialect in their teaching.

shootoutStatements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.
